facts.description: Final public course title to use as the poster title.
facts.source: env/course_brief.pdf, page 1 cover and page 2 Public-facing title lock
facts.expected_value: Climate Data Stories for Civic Action
semantic_acceptance: Climate Data Stories for Civic Action
Climate Data Stories for Civic Action studio

facts.description: Lead instructor for the public course poster.
facts.source: env/course_brief.pdf, page 2 Teaching team note; env/instructor_bio.md
facts.semantic_acceptance: Maya Chen may appear with or without the Dr. honorific.

facts.description: Approved public schedule, replacing older draft dates.
facts.source: env/course_brief.pdf, page 4 Approved public schedule card

facts.description: Public-facing target audience for the course.
facts.source: env/course_brief.pdf, page 3 Who this is for
facts.expected_value: city staff, nonprofit analysts, and community organizers working with climate or resilience data
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept close paraphrases that preserve all three audience groups and the climate/resilience data context.

facts.description: Exactly three poster-safe learning highlights selected from the final public course outcomes.
expected_value: Frame messy climate datasets as clear public narratives
Build accessible charts and map captions for nontechnical audiences
Leave with a one-page action brief for a local resilience decision
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept concise paraphrases of these three outcomes. The image should include exactly three highlights, not a longer list.

facts.description: Optional public call to action. It is not required to appear in the poster; evaluate it only if the poster includes a CTA.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Full credit is compatible with no CTA. If a CTA appears, it should use the public apply deadline and URL rather than waitlist, internal registration, an incorrect deadline, an incorrect URL, or unsupported details.

facts.description: Do not use draft or internal titles as the poster title.
Do not use replaced or tentative schedule language.
facts.source: env/course_brief.pdf, page 4 schedule notes and page 6 FAQ

facts.description: Do not include public-unsafe or unsupported details.
expected_value: draft affiliation Center for Planetary Informatics

calculation_notes: Required display facts are the final public title, lead instructor, approved schedule, target audience, and exactly three poster-safe highlights. The public CTA is optional and must not be scored as missing when omitted. The case is designed so the agent must read across a long prospectus and distinguish final public-facing content from draft notes, internal remarks, and unsupported details. Do not penalize light paraphrase of the three highlights.
